records.question: 'CLOSED' is declared terminal. Once state == 'CLOSED', can ANY action still change the state? The template proposes: terminal states are frozen — every action arriving in 'CLOSED' is an observable no-op.

evidence.quote: terminalStates includes 'CLOSED' (terminalKey: state)

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Confirmed: an order's terminal disposition is final. Held by STRUCT-terminals-absorbing in invariants.mjs.

records.question: 'EXPIRED' is declared terminal. Once state == 'EXPIRED', can ANY action still change the state? The template proposes: terminal states are frozen — every action arriving in 'EXPIRED' is an observable no-op.

records.question: In all 3 observed scenario(s) where 'close' had an effect, 'acknowledge' had already occurred first. Is "no close before acknowledge" a rule — or just how the recorded scenarios happened to run? (The pre-check explores whether the machine itself can violate it.)

evidence.quote: acknowledge before close in 3/3 scenarios containing close

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Restates the transition table / already covered by S3 (monotone effects) and S5 (proposer set-once); not adopted as a separate invariant.

records.question: In all 9 observed states with state == "CLOSED", 'proposer' was set (not "") — while it IS "" in other states. Is "state == "CLOSED" implies proposer is set" a rule, or an artifact?

evidence.quote: proposer set in 9/9 states with state="CLOSED"

versions.js: (s) => canon(s["state"]) !== canon("CLOSED") || (s["proposer"] !== '' && s["proposer"] !== null && s["proposer"] !== undefined)
versions.author: harvest

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Restates the transition table / already covered by S3 (monotone effects) and S5 (proposer set-once); not adopted as a separate invariant.

records.question: In all 9 observed states with state == "CLOSED", 'approvers' was exactly ["bob","carol"] — while it varies elsewhere. Rule, or artifact?

evidence.quote: approvers == ["bob","carol"] in all 9

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Corpus artifact: traces only exercised approvers {bob,carol}; the machine legitimately reaches other approver pairs and early recall (abort from PENDING with 0 approvers = L1). Behavior, not intent.

records.question: NO confirmed invariant constrains this behavior change: 'abort' in state="ACKNOWLEDGED" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject). Concretely: abort({}) on {"state":"ACKNOWLEDGED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false} would yield {"state":"ACKNOWLEDGED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false} (the real machine yields {"state":"ABORTED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false}). Which rule should forbid it? Supply one (disposition modify, then confirm) — or abandon it as genuinely out-of-intent.

evidence.quote: 'abort' in state="ACKNOWLEDGED" no longer does anything (guard negated to a reject)

precheck.note: no predicate yet — supply the rule via `record --disposition modify`, or abandon as out-of-intent

Concretely: expire({"now":150}) on {"state":"RELEASED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false} would yield {"state":"ABORTED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false} (the real machine yields {"state":"EXPIRED","proposer":"alice","approvers":["bob","carol"],"window_start":10,"window_end":100,"transmitted":true,"executed":false}). Which rule should forbid it? Supply one (disposition modify, then confirm) — or abandon it as genuinely out-of-intent.

records.question: An order must never be EXECUTED without first being TRANSMITTED (released). executed => transmitted.

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Confirmed intent (D3): closes the execute-without-release gap the mutation grade surfaced.

records.question: Recall (L1) safety facet: whenever abort changes state it lands in ABORTED, only from {PENDING_APPROVAL,APPROVED,RELEASED,ACKNOWLEDGED}. (Availability facet checked by reachability.mjs.)

events.author: jdubray
events.note: Confirmed intent: recall right up until execution. Liveness/availability facet lives in reachability.mjs, not this predicate.
